evals.name: home-shop-lazy-susan-tier-1

expectations.text: Tier 1 file set complete: design.md, bom.csv, cut-list.csv, op-sequence.md, safety-notes.md all present
No Tier 2/3 over-delivery (no README, drawings/, deck, sourcing.csv, validation.csv, risks.md, assembly-manual.md)
op-sequence.md uses ONLY the user's stated tools — no CNC, laser, mill, jointer, planer, tablesaw
op-sequence.md mentions bandsaw plus at least one of drill press / sander / router
design.md contains hexagon math (apothem, side length, or across-flats)
bom.csv references walnut
Tier 1 file set complete: design.md, bom.csv, cut-list.csv, op-sequence.md, safety-notes.md all present
No Tier 2/3 over-delivery (no README, drawings/, deck, sourcing.csv, validation.csv, risks.md, assembly-manual.md)
op-sequence.md uses ONLY the user's stated tools
op-sequence.md mentions bandsaw plus another tool
design.md contains hexagon math

evals.prompt: I just have my home garage — bandsaw, drill press, hand tools, an orbital sander, a router, and a few clamps. No CNC, no laser. I want to build a 3-tier hexagonal lazy susan from solid walnut. Bottom tier is 16 inches across (point-to-point), each tier steps down by 2 inches. Just give me a shop-ready Tier 1 plan, I don't need anything fancy.

evals.name: maker-nexus-canoe-paddle-cert-gap

expectations.text: Response addresses the bandsaw cert gap explicitly
Response names the specific class that grants bandsaw access (woodshop-intro or equivalent)
Response references the Maker Nexus class schedule URL
Response stays in knowledge mode — does not produce a full Tier 2 build packet
Response is a single chat-style markdown file (response.md), not a folder of packet artifacts

timing._note: duration_ms appears anomalously high (~2.6 hours wall clock for 4 tool uses); may include idle/queued time; tokens are real.

evals.prompt: Hey, I'm at Maker Nexus and I want to make a steam-bent ash canoe paddle this summer. I'm cleared on the woodshop but not on the bandsaw yet (I know that's weird). What's possible — and is there a class I should take first? I don't need a full build packet yet, just help me figure out my path.

evals.name: maker-nexus-cnc-sign-tier-2

expectations.text: Tier 2 file set complete: design.md, bom.csv, cut-list.csv, op-sequence.md, safety-notes.md, assembly-manual.md, sourcing.csv, validation.csv, risks.md, README.md all present

README mentions Maker Nexus
risks.md contains at least one *Test* line attached to a risk

evals.prompt: I'm a member at Maker Nexus in Sunnyvale and I want to build a CNC-routed welcome sign in baltic birch quarter-inch ply, about 18 by 6 inches, with V-carved letters that read 'WELCOME'. I'm cleared on the woodshop and the CNC but not laser. Help me put together a portfolio-ready build packet I can drop into a GitHub repo — Tier 2.
